upstream_state.reason: The Indonesian case report is concrete enough to ask qualified owners how the route worked, but not enough to infer Case-001 access, eligibility, referral, travel, import, purchase, or treatment selection.

source_checks.source_label: Indonesian pediatric thalassemia gene-therapy case report
source_checks.source_role: Reports screening, apheresis and cell manufacturing, conditioning, cell infusion, and six-month follow-up for one Indonesian pediatric case using autologous CD34+ HSPCs modified with a lentiviral beta-globin vector.

Current guideline chapter explaining gene addition and gene editing as specialist-owned TDT approaches requiring autologous HSC collection, ex vivo modification, myeloablative conditioning, reinfusion, and follow-up.
Official current U.S. gene-editing benchmark for TDT, including STN 125787 for patients aged 2 years and older.
Official U.S. gene-addition benchmark for adult and pediatric beta-thalassemia patients requiring regular RBC transfusions.
Singapore regulator context for cell, tissue, and gene therapy clinical trials, notification or registration, dealing, adverse-event, and defect-reporting obligations.
Current secondary report quoting BPOM leadership on ATMP regulation, GMP capacity, scientific evidence, process consistency, and long-term safety monitoring.
Access-hurdle review noting gene-therapy efficacy signals but emphasizing safety, commercialization, and cost barriers.

owner_questions.one_question: Which private record domains must be complete before any advanced-therapy relevance question is clinically meaningful?
source_labels: Indonesian pediatric thalassemia gene-therapy case report
owner_questions.blocked_jump: Do not infer diagnosis, genotype interpretation, conditioning fitness, or therapy selection from the public case report.

owner_questions.one_question: Was the reported route local, cross-border, research-only, commercial, or otherwise exceptional, and which parts are reproducible for other Indonesian patients?
source_labels: Indonesian pediatric thalassemia gene-therapy case report
owner_questions.blocked_jump: Do not treat a single published case as a referral path or access guarantee.

owner_questions.one_question: Which authorization, assessment, import, research, or hospital-governance route applied to the reported case, if any?
owner_questions.blocked_jump: Do not convert foreign approvals or ATMP policy context into Indonesian legal access instructions.

owner_questions.one_question: Which collection, vector or cell-processing, release-testing, chain-of-identity, chain-of-custody, and product-return steps were required?
source_labels: Indonesian pediatric thalassemia gene-therapy case report
owner_questions.blocked_jump: Do not request samples, contact labs, or infer manufacturing feasibility from public text.

owner_questions.one_question: What payer, sponsor, charity, self-pay, hospital, or exceptional funding path made the reported case possible?
source_labels: Indonesian pediatric thalassemia gene-therapy case report
owner_questions.blocked_jump: Do not treat a reported case as affordability, reimbursement, or procurement evidence.

owner_questions.one_question: What follow-up protocol is required beyond the six-month report, including blood, organ-iron, adverse-event, and long-term safety surveillance?
source_labels: Indonesian pediatric thalassemia gene-therapy case report
owner_questions.blocked_jump: Do not call six-month transfusion independence a durable cure without qualified long-term follow-up review.

decision_rule: Use the Indonesian case report to ask one owner-bounded question at a time. Do not move Case-001 out of branch_review_handoff_packet_incomplete until private packet readiness and qualified clinician review change.
